danger_signals.visual_form: NPCs rendered in ice/crystal state, immobilized
danger_signals.meaning: Curse/magical threat active; world in danger state

danger_signals.visual_form: Empty terrain, no flora, muted color palette, wasteland appearance
danger_signals.meaning: World not yet resurrected; life absent; hostile environment

danger_signals.visual_form: Jagged crystalline formations, flowing lava visual effects
danger_signals.meaning: Underworld hazards; inhospitable environment; active danger zones

danger_signals.visual_form: Visual corruption/degradation on large tree entity
danger_signals.meaning: Biological threat; contamination present; healing needed

danger_signals.visual_form: Visual barrier/dimensional crevasse between Lightside and Darkside
danger_signals.meaning: World-state separation; threat containment; passage requires action

safety_signals.visual_form: Populated settlement with standing structures, NPCs present (when not cursed)
safety_signals.meaning: Safe haven; home base; refuge from underworld hazards

safety_signals.visual_form: Landmasses emerge from sea; terrain becomes walkable and defined
safety_signals.meaning: Progress toward world salvation; danger decreasing; habitability increasing

safety_signals.visual_form: Trees, plants, birds, animals rendered in environment
safety_signals.meaning: Life restored; world healing; safety conditions improving

safety_signals.visual_form: NPCs inhabiting towns, structures rebuilt, economic activity visible
safety_signals.meaning: Civilization restored; stability achieved; human presence indicating safety

safety_signals.visual_form: Elder NPC visually present in Crysta or appearing in dreams
safety_signals.meaning: Divine/authoritative support; direction clear; path forward sanctioned

faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Surface world (Lightside); growth-oriented environments; areas of resurrection

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Yomi (friendly demon, contained in box); dark/underworld aesthetic
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Beruga (scientist antagonist with Paradise vision); zombie-converted entities
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Underworld (Darkside); decline-oriented frozen wasteland; magma and crystal formations

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Ark (protagonist); Elle (human/princess connection); invented technology symbols
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Asmodeus virus victims; non-immortal life marked for elimination
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Developing cities; technological infrastructure; populated settlements; French village and NY-style locations

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Cryogenic sleep chamber; immortality/zombie conversion imagery; sterile/perfect world aesthetic
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Zombie-converted humans; virus-infected entities; life forms targeted for elimination
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Advanced technological areas; locations of scientific progress; sealed/controlled environments

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Crysta village with frozen NPCs; dark/icy underworld aesthetics; dimensional barrier visible
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: NPCs immobilized; Ark alone capable of movement; Elder guides from behind scenes; no external travel possible

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Landmasses emerge from sea; underworld terrain becomes more navigable; visual expansion of traversable world
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Frozen villagers thaw; world opens for exploration; Ark gains access to surface (Lightside)

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Flora appears (trees, plants); fauna appears (birds, animals); color palette shifts from barren to verdant; cities rebuild
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: NPCs repopulate world; trade/economic activity resumes; Elle encountered as Lightside twin; technological development begins

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Full cities visible; technological infrastructure (ground-breaking inventions); populated regions with human activity; industrial/modern aesthetics emerging
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Humans develop technology; cities expand; Elle becomes integral to world; Beruga awakens and reveals alternate vision

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Contrast between perfected technological zones and zones marked for elimination; possible visual corruption or sterility; viral infection indicators
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Beruga proposes zombie immortality; Asmodeus virus threatens 'insignificant' life; conflict between progress and survival

environmental_storytelling.technique: Progression through visual desolation to abundance
environmental_storytelling.example: Barren landscape → resurrected continents → populated cities; player witnesses world state change through environment alone
Frozen underworld wasteland vs. surface world recovery; visual geography reinforces thematic opposition of growth vs. decline
Ra afflicted by parasite shown as visual degradation; healing Ra triggers ecological resurrection; cause-effect communicated visually
Ruined settlements → rebuilt towns → advanced cities with technology; player observes civilization's reconstruction through structural progression
Frozen Crysta villagers → thawed citizens → repopulated world; NPC activity density reflects resurrection progress
Crevasse between Lightside/Darkside visually represents separation; passage signals major narrative/world-state transition

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Only mobile entity; all others frozen; bears sole responsibility for world resurrection
Travels across expanding world; solves environmental problems (parasite, revival tasks); assists NPC progression
Engages with developed society; encounters political/romantic elements (Princess Elle); faces antagonist ideology
Guides Ark; provides dream sequences; directs world resurrection tasks; represents divine authority
Complete immobilization; no interaction; visual status as victims
Resume normal animation; become interactive; populate world; indicate successful resurrection
Isolated; limited interaction; visual/behavioral indication of psychological damage
Develops relationship with Ark; becomes active in world; grows closer over time; indicates emotional recovery
Proposes Paradise ideology; threatens life forms deemed 'insignificant'; uses Asmodeus virus; represents antagonistic force
Undead state; serve Beruga's vision; represent loss of humanity; visual corruption from normal NPC state
Visually degraded; immobile/afflicted; blocks world progression
Resurrects all plant life; enables world traversal; becomes beneficial environmental force

notes: Terranigma uses a dual-world system (Lightside/Darkside) to communicate world-state visually and narratively. The game's primary manifestation mechanic is progressive resurrection: barren → life → civilization. Danger is signaled through environmental desolation, crystallization (curse imagery), and structural/biological corruption. Safety emerges through populated settlements, restored ecology, and NPC activity. Faction conflict is presented as ideological: God/Devil abstraction (growth vs. decline) becomes concrete in Beruga's Paradise proposal (immortality via zombification and selective life elimination). Visual storytelling prioritizes environmental progression over explicit UI—the player witnesses world recovery through landscape, architecture, and NPC presence changes. The game's paradigm 'other' reflects its unique action-RPG/resurrection-narrative hybrid nature, distinct from typical fantasy or sci-fi frameworks.
sources_used: tcrf (The Cutting Room Floor) - Terranigma article
